description: A national mission for financial inclusion to ensure access to financial services, namely Banking/Savings & Deposit Accounts, Remittance, Credit, Insurance, and Pension in an affordable manner. Launched on 28th August 2014, the scheme has been one of the biggest financial inclusion initiatives in the world.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen above 10 years of age who does not have a bank account. Minor accounts can be opened with a guardian. Both urban and rural citizens are eligible. No minimum balance requirement.
benefits: Zero balance savings account with RuPay debit card. Accidental insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh (enhanced from Rs. 1 lakh). Life insurance cover of Rs. 30,000 for accounts opened between 15 Aug 2014 to 31 Jan 2015. Overdraft facility of up to Rs. 10,000 for one account per household after satisfactory operation for 6 months. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of government subsidies.
interest_rate: Savings account interest rate as applicable by the respective bank, typically 4% per annum. No charges for zero balance maintenance.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or any officially valid document (Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport, NREGA Job Card). If address differs from Aadhaar, a self-certification is sufficient.

description: A scheme launched to provide loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. The loans are classified as MUDRA loans under three categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishore (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5 lakh), and Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10 lakh). The scheme supports income-generating manufacturing, trading, and service sector activities.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm income generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector whose credit need is less than Rs. 10 lakh. Includes shopkeepers, vendors, artisans, and small manufacturers. No collateral required for loans up to Rs. 10 lakh.
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to Rs. 10 lakh through banks, NBFCs, and MFIs. Three-tier categorization allows tailored loan amounts. MUDRA Card for working capital requirements. Reduced interest rates compared to informal lending. No processing fee for Shishu category loans.
interest_rate: Interest rates vary by lending institution. Typically: Shishu: 10-12% p.a., Kishore: 11-14% p.a., Tarun: 12-16% p.a. Rates are at the discretion of the lending institution. Women entrepreneurs may receive preferential rates.
documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar, PAN card), Address proof, Business plan or project report, Passport-size photographs, Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC) if applicable, Quotation of machinery/equipment to be purchased.

description: An accident insurance scheme offering accidental death and disability cover. The scheme is available to people in the age group of 18 to 70 years having a savings bank account. The risk coverage is for one year from 1st June to 31st May and is renewable annually. It is one of the world's most affordable accident insurance schemes.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-70 years with a savings bank account. Aadhaar is the primary KYC. NRIs with eligible bank accounts can also enroll. One person can join through one bank account only.
benefits: Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death and full disability. Rs. 1 lakh for partial disability. Annual premium of only Rs. 20 (auto-debited from bank account). Coverage period: 1st June to 31st May each year.
interest_rate: Not applicable — this is an insurance product. Annual premium: Rs. 20 per annum per member, auto-debited from the subscriber's bank account.
documents_required: Savings bank account, Aadhaar number for KYC, Consent-cum-declaration form.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: A government-backed life insurance scheme offering life cover of Rs. 2 lakh at a premium of Rs. 436 per annum. Available to people in the age group of 18-50 years having a savings bank account. The risk coverage is for one year from 1st June to 31st May, renewable annually. It provides pure term life insurance cover.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-50 years with a savings bank account. Those who join before age 50 can continue to have risk of life cover up to age 55, subject to premium payment. Aadhaar is the primary KYC.
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh in case of death due to any reason. Annual premium of Rs. 436 (revised from Rs. 330), auto-debited from bank account. Coverage period: 1st June to 31st May each year. No medical examination required.
interest_rate: Not applicable — this is an insurance product. Annual premium: Rs. 436 per annum per member.
documents_required: Savings bank account, Aadhaar number for KYC, Consent-cum-declaration form, Nominee details.

description: A government-backed pension scheme aimed at workers in the unorganized sector. The scheme provides a guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 per month from the age of 60 years, depending on the contribution made. The Central Government also co-contributes 50% of the subscriber's contribution or Rs. 1,000 per annum, whichever is lower, to eligible subscribers.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-40 years. Must have a savings bank account. Should not be a member of any statutory social security scheme. Should not be an income tax payer. The contribution period is minimum 20 years.
benefits: Guaranteed pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 per month after age 60. Government co-contribution for eligible subscribers who joined before 31st March 2016. Spouse continues to receive pension after subscriber's death. Entire corpus returned to nominee after both subscriber and spouse pass away. Tax benefits under Section 80CCD(1B) of Income Tax Act.
interest_rate: Not applicable — pension scheme. Monthly contributions range from Rs. 42 to Rs. 1,454 depending on entry age and desired pension amount. Lower entry age means lower monthly contribution for the same pension.
documents_required: Savings bank account, Aadhaar number, Mobile number linked to bank account, Nominee details.

description: A small savings scheme under the Beti Bachao Beti Padhao campaign for the welfare of girl children. The account can be opened in the name of a girl child from the time of birth until she attains the age of 10 years. The scheme offers the highest interest rate among all small savings instruments and provides triple tax exemption (EEE status).
eligibility: A girl child below 10 years of age. Maximum two accounts for two different girl children (exception for twins/triplets). Natural or legal guardian can open the account. The girl child must be an Indian resident.
benefits: Highest interest rate among government savings schemes (currently 8.2% p.a., compounded annually). Triple tax exemption: deposit, interest, and maturity — all tax-free under Section 80C. Minimum deposit Rs. 250 per year, maximum Rs. 1.5 lakh per year. Account matures after 21 years from opening or marriage of girl child after age 18. Partial withdrawal (50%) allowed after girl turns 18 for education.
interest_rate: 8.2% per annum (Q1 FY2025-26), compounded annually. Rate is reviewed quarterly by the Ministry of Finance and is consistently among the highest in government savings instruments.
documents_required: Birth certificate of girl child, Identity proof of guardian (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport), Address proof of guardian, Passport-size photographs of guardian and girl child.

description: A savings certificate scheme that doubles the invested amount in a fixed time period. It is a safe investment instrument for those who do not want to take market risk. KVP can be purchased from any post office or authorized banks. The certificates are available in denominations of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 5,000, Rs. 10,000, and Rs. 50,000.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen above 18 years of age. A minor's account can be opened through a guardian. Joint accounts (up to 3 adults) are allowed. Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) and NRIs are NOT eligible.
benefits: Guaranteed doubling of investment. No upper limit on investment (minimum Rs. 1,000). Can be used as collateral for loans. Transferable from one person to another. Encashable after 2 years 6 months (lock-in period). Nomination facility available.
interest_rate: 7.5% per annum (Q1 FY2025-26), compounded annually. The maturity period is 115 months (9 years 7 months) for the investment to double. Rate is reviewed quarterly.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card (mandatory for investments above Rs. 50,000), Address proof, Passport-size photographs, KVP application form.

description: Government securities denominated in grams of gold, issued by the Reserve Bank of India on behalf of the Government of India. They act as a substitute for holding physical gold. The bonds are issued in tranches throughout the year. Investors are assured of the market price of gold at the time of maturity and periodical interest.
eligibility: Indian residents including individuals, HUFs, trusts, universities, and charitable institutions. Minimum investment: 1 gram of gold. Maximum investment: 4 kg per individual per fiscal year, 20 kg for trusts. NRIs are NOT eligible.
benefits: Fixed interest of 2.50% per annum on initial investment, paid semi-annually. Capital gains tax exemption on redemption for individuals. No storage cost or risk of theft. Can be used as collateral for loans. Listed on stock exchanges for early exit. Eliminates purity and making charges associated with physical gold.
interest_rate: 2.50% per annum on the initial investment amount, payable semi-annually. Additionally, the redemption price is linked to the prevailing gold price, providing capital appreciation potential. Maturity period: 8 years, with early exit option after 5 years.
documents_required: PAN Card (mandatory), Aadhaar Card, Passport-size photographs, KYC documents as required by the issuing bank/post office.

description: A government-backed savings scheme exclusively for senior citizens in India. It offers one of the highest interest rates among small savings instruments, providing regular income through quarterly interest payouts. The scheme is available at post offices and authorized banks. It is particularly suitable for retirees seeking safe, regular income.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 60 years and above. Citizens aged 55-60 who have retired on superannuation or under VRS can also invest (within 1 month of receiving retirement benefits). Retired defence personnel aged 50+ are eligible. NRIs and HUFs are NOT eligible. Joint account allowed with spouse only.
benefits: High interest rate (currently 8.2% p.a.) paid quarterly. Investment up to Rs. 30 lakh (enhanced from Rs. 15 lakh). Tax deduction under Section 80C up to Rs. 1.5 lakh. Premature withdrawal allowed after 1 year with penalty. Account can be extended for 3 years after maturity. Nomination facility available.
interest_rate: 8.2% per annum (Q1 FY2025-26), payable quarterly on 1st April, 1st July, 1st October, and 1st January. TDS is deducted if interest exceeds Rs. 50,000 per annum. Rate is reviewed quarterly by the Ministry of Finance.
documents_required: Age proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Birth Certificate), Address proof, Passport-size photographs, Retirement proof (for those aged 55-60), SCSS account opening form, Cheque/DD for the deposit amount.

description: A major credit-linked subsidy programme aimed at generating self-employment opportunities through establishment of micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector. It is implemented by the Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) at the national level, with state directorates at the state level. The scheme merges the former PMRY and REGP schemes.
eligibility: Any individual above 18 years of age. Minimum 8th grade pass for manufacturing projects above Rs. 10 lakh and service projects above Rs. 5 lakh. Self-help groups, cooperative societies, and charitable trusts are also eligible. Existing units and those already availed of PMRY/REGP or any other government subsidy are NOT eligible.
benefits: Subsidy of 15-35% of project cost for setting up micro enterprises. Manufacturing sector: projects up to Rs. 50 lakh. Service sector: projects up to Rs. 20 lakh. Higher subsidy for SC/ST/OBC/minorities/women/ex-servicemen/PwD (25-35%). Remaining amount as bank loan. No income ceiling for assistance.
interest_rate: Bank loan interest rates apply for the non-subsidy portion, typically 10-14% p.a. depending on the bank. The subsidy portion (15-35%) is a one-time grant, not a loan. The margin money (own contribution) is 5-10% of the project cost.
documents_required: Identity proof, Age proof, Educational qualification certificate, Project report, Caste certificate (if applicable), Special category certificate (if applicable), Rural area certificate (if claiming higher subsidy).
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

description: Facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore to at least one Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe borrower and at least one Woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise in manufacturing, services, or the trading sector. The scheme is complementary to the Start-Up India initiative.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or Women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age. The enterprise must be a greenfield (new) enterprise. In case of non-individual enterprises, 51% shareholding and controlling stake should be held by SC/ST or Woman entrepreneur. Borrower should not be a defaulter to any bank/financial institution.
benefits: Composite loan of Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore covering term loan and working capital. Repayment period up to 7 years with maximum moratorium of 18 months. Margin money: 25% of the project cost (can be met through convergence with PMEGP/MUDRA). RuPee portal for online application and portal-based monitoring.
interest_rate: Interest rate: Base rate of the respective bank (MCLR) + 3% + tenor premium. Typically ranges from 10-14% p.a. depending on the bank and credit profile. Rate varies by lending institution.
documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar, PAN), Address proof, Caste certificate (for SC/ST), Business plan or project report, Photographs, IT returns for last 2 years (if available), Asset-liability statement.
ministry: Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance

description: A long-term savings scheme backed by the Government of India with attractive interest rates and tax benefits. PPF is one of the most popular and safest long-term investment options in India. The scheme has a lock-in period of 15 years and offers EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax status, meaning contributions, interest earned, and maturity proceeds are all tax-free.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen (resident). One account per person. Account for minor can be opened by guardian. NRIs who opened PPF while being residents can continue until maturity but cannot extend. HUFs are NOT eligible (since 2005).
benefits: Complete tax exemption under EEE status (Section 80C deduction up to Rs. 1.5 lakh). Interest earned and maturity amount are tax-free. Minimum deposit: Rs. 500 per year, Maximum: Rs. 1.5 lakh per year. Partial withdrawal allowed from 7th year. Loan facility available from 3rd to 6th year. Extension in blocks of 5 years after maturity. Attachment-proof from court decrees (sovereign guarantee).
interest_rate: 7.1% per annum (Q1 FY2025-26), compounded annually. Interest is calculated on the lowest balance between the 5th and last day of each month. Rate is reviewed and announced quarterly by the Ministry of Finance.
documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Voter ID), Address proof, Passport-size photographs, PPF account opening form, Nomination form.

description: The Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC), a wholly-owned subsidiary of RBI, provides deposit insurance coverage to all depositors of insured banks. Each depositor in a bank is insured up to a maximum of Rs. 5 lakh for both principal and interest amount held across all branches of the same bank. This is a critical safety net for the banking system.
eligibility: All depositors of all commercial banks, regional rural banks, local area banks, cooperative banks, and foreign bank branches operating in India are automatically covered. No separate enrollment needed. Coverage is automatic upon opening a deposit account.
benefits: Insurance cover up to Rs. 5 lakh per depositor per bank (enhanced from Rs. 1 lakh in February 2020). Covers savings, fixed, current, and recurring deposits. Coverage includes both principal and interest. Interim payments within 90 days if a bank is placed under moratorium. No action required from depositors — coverage is automatic.
interest_rate: Not applicable — this is a regulatory insurance mechanism. The insurance premium is paid by the insured banks (not depositors) at 12 paise per Rs. 100 of assessable deposits per annum.
documents_required: No separate documentation required. Coverage is automatic for all deposit accounts in insured banks.

description: RBI mandates that all scheduled commercial banks must allocate a certain percentage of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) to priority sectors. This ensures adequate institutional credit flow to sectors that significantly contribute to employment and GDP but may not have the same access to bank credit as other sectors. Priority sectors include agriculture, MSME, education, housing, social infrastructure, and renewable energy.
eligibility: Applicability varies by sector: Agriculture loans up to Rs. 2 crore, MSME loans as per MSMED Act definitions, Education loans up to Rs. 20 lakh, Housing loans up to Rs. 35 lakh (metro) / Rs. 25 lakh (non-metro), Social infrastructure loans up to Rs. 5 crore, Renewable energy loans up to Rs. 30 crore, Weaker sections as defined by RBI.
benefits: Easier access to credit for priority sectors. Banks are required to lend 40% of ANBC to priority sectors (75% for regional rural banks and small finance banks). Sub-targets: 18% for agriculture, 7.5% for micro enterprises, 12% for weaker sections. Shortfall must be deposited with NABARD/SIDBI/NHB. Interest subvention schemes may apply for certain categories.
interest_rate: Interest rates vary by sector and borrower category. Agriculture: typically 7-9% p.a. (with interest subvention of 3% for prompt repayment in crop loans up to Rs. 3 lakh). MSME: 8-14% p.a. Education: 8-11% p.a. Housing: linked to repo rate (typically repo + 2-3%).
documents_required: Varies by sector. Generally includes: identity proof, address proof, income proof, project report/purpose statement, land records (agriculture), admission letter (education), property documents (housing).

description: The Direct Benefit Transfer initiative ensures that government subsidies and benefits are transferred directly into the bank accounts of beneficiaries. This eliminates intermediaries, reduces corruption, and ensures targeted delivery of welfare benefits. DBT leverages the JAM (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) trinity for seamless transfers. Over 300 government schemes now use DBT for benefit distribution.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen who is a beneficiary of government subsidy or welfare schemes. Must have a bank account (preferably Jan Dhan account) linked with Aadhaar. Mobile number should be registered with the bank and linked to Aadhaar. Applicable to beneficiaries of LPG subsidy, MGNREGA, scholarships, pensions, PM-KISAN, and other government schemes.
benefits: Direct transfer to bank account eliminates leakage and corruption. Real-time tracking of benefits. Reduced delays in benefit disbursement. Portability across banks through Aadhaar-based payment bridge. Transparency and accountability in government spending. Estimated savings of over Rs. 2.73 lakh crore through DBT since inception.
interest_rate: Not applicable — DBT is a transfer mechanism, not a financial product. Benefits received via DBT are subject to the terms of the respective government scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card (mandatory for most DBT schemes), Bank account details, Mobile number linked to Aadhaar, Scheme-specific documents as required.
